Name: Planar Unit-Distance Discovery
Description: A CMCC semantic mirror of the planar unit-distance theorem neighborhood. Every mathematical object that participates in the chain from Q to U(n) is promoted to its OWN first-class table, and every intermediate quantity (distance squared, edge count, density exponent, witness-consistency check, bound exponent, anchor match) is a first-class calculated/lookup/aggregation field on the entity to which it belongs. Nothing is hidden in a property bag, an expression tree, or a generic relation row. The Pythagorean test: if a mathematician would name it, it has its own column.

__meta__.Description: Project-level metadata that travels with the rulebook: tagline, motif, narrative descriptions, substrate list, signature rows, etc. One row per metadata key. Use ValueType to interpret StringValue vs JsonValue.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: The metadata key (e.g. 'tagline', 'motif_palette', 'substrates'). Unique within the table.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Identifier for this metadata entry. Mirrors MetaKey so the row is addressable by Name like every other table.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: How to interpret the value columns: 'string' (use StringValue), 'object' (parse JsonValue as JSON object), 'array' (parse JsonValue as JSON array).

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Plain string value. Populated when ValueType == 'string'; null otherwise.

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: JSON-encoded value. Populated when ValueType == 'object' or 'array'; null when ValueType == 'string'.

data.Name: tagline
data.StringValue: A semantic mirror of the planar unit-distance neighborhood — every quantity in the chain from Q to U(n) is a first-class table.

data.Name: description_rich
data.StringValue: A CMCC semantic mirror of the planar unit-distance theorem neighborhood. Every mathematical object that participates in the chain from Q to U(n) is promoted to its OWN first-class table, and every intermediate quantity (distance squared, edge count, density exponent, witness-consistency check, bound exponent, anchor match) is a first-class calculated/lookup/aggregation field on the entity to which it belongs. Nothing is hidden in a property bag, an expression tree, or a generic relation row. The Pythagorean test: if a mathematician would name it, it has its own column.

data.Name: use_cases
data.JsonValue: ["Make a frontier-math result expressible as a rulebook — every object (points, pairs, graphs, lattices, fields, asymptotic bounds) is a typed table, not a comment.", "Demonstrate aggregation-heavy CMCC: edge counts, density exponents, witness-consistency checks all derive as calculated/aggregation fields, not as opaque code.", "Show that a research-grade ontology and a business CRUD ontology share the same primitive set — no special-casing for math."]

data.Name: journal_seed
data.StringValue: If a theorem about points in the plane can be a rulebook with the same primitives that drive an invoicing app, the conjecture that ‘every domain is a rulebook’ has another receipt.

Domains.Description: Table: Domains. Top-level mathematical neighborhoods used to group entities for navigation. Not load-bearing for computation — purely a tag.

data.Description: Points, configurations, distance, unit-distance predicate.
Unit-distance graphs and edge counts.
Number fields, rings of integers, prime ideals.
Minkowski lattices, short vectors, planar projection.
Construction families, growth sequences, lower bounds.
Theorem anchors and their bounds.

Contexts.Description: Table: Contexts. Mathematical worlds in which assertions are interpreted. Used as the valid-time context for facts (e.g. 'this lattice fact is valid in the Euclidean plane with standard metric').

data.Description: Standard two-dimensional Euclidean geometry.

data.AssumptionsJSON: {"object":"finite point sets in R^2","edge_rule":"distance equals one"}
data.Description: Maximize the number of unit-distance pairs among n points in the plane.
The neighborhood anchored by the superlinear lower bound.

Points.Description: Table: Points. A single point in R^2. Coordinates are raw; everything else lives on PointPairs / PointSets.

schema.name: Context
schema.Description: Which mathematical context the coordinates are interpreted in.

PointSets.Description: Table: PointSets. A finite configuration P ⊂ R^2. Aggregations count membership and unit-distance pairs; calculated fields derive density and exponent estimates. The Erdős unit-distance question lives on this table — 'how many unit pairs can n points have?' is literally `UnitDistancePairCount` for varying `PointCount`.

schema.name: PointCount
schema.Description: n in U(n).

schema.name: UnitDistancePairCount
schema.Description: e(G(P)) — the number of edges in the induced unit-distance graph.

schema.name: MaxPossibleEdges
schema.Description: n choose 2 — the trivial upper bound on UnitDistancePairCount.

schema.name: EdgeDensity
schema.Description: Fraction of point pairs that are unit pairs.

schema.name: DensityExponentEstimate
schema.Description: The exponent c such that UnitDistancePairCount ≈ PointCount^c. Lower bounds for U(n) are claims that this exponent is ≥ some target for arbitrarily large PointCount.

data.Description: Three vertices of an equilateral triangle with side 1.
Four vertices of an axis-aligned unit square. Note: only 4 of 6 pairs are unit; the diagonals are sqrt(2).

The σ_1 projection of {0, 1, ζ_7, ζ_7^2, …, ζ_7^6} from Z[ζ_7]'s Minkowski lattice: 7 unit-circle vertices plus the origin. Produces 7 spoke unit-distance pairs (center↔vertex); no vertex-vertex pairs are unit (closest chord is 2sin(π/7) ≈ 0.868). Demonstrates the Q(ζ_7) chain end-to-end but does NOT itself achieve density exponent > 1.

PointSetMembers.Description: Table: PointSetMembers. Junction: which Points belong to which PointSet. Promoted to a first-class entity because membership is itself a fact that may carry context.

PointPairs.Description: Table: PointPairs. An ordered pair (PointA, PointB) drawn from the same PointSet. Every step the mathematician would name is here as its own column — DeltaX, DeltaXSquared, DistanceSquared, Distance, DistanceFromUnit, IsUnitDistance. The reader can audit each step.

schema.name: PointAX
schema.Description: Lookup of PointA.X.

schema.name: DistanceSquared
schema.Description: Pythagoras, made explicit. Promoted because every downstream check uses it directly.

schema.name: Tolerance
schema.Description: Numerical tolerance for the unit-distance check.

schema.name: IsUnitDistance
schema.Description: The unit-distance predicate — the entire problem turns on this boolean.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Free-text annotation for this row.

data.Description: Adjacent-vertex chord: length 2sin(π/7) ≈ 0.868 — NOT unit.
Two-apart chord: length 2sin(2π/7) ≈ 1.564 — NOT unit.

UnitDistanceGraphs.Description: Table: UnitDistanceGraphs. The induced graph G(P) for a PointSet P. Has one row per PointSet that we choose to project into graph-form. Vertex count is the point count, edge count is the unit-distance pair count — these are not duplicated facts, they are lookups.

schema.name: DensityExponentEstimate
schema.Description: Witness for the exponent in U(n) ≥ n^c, finite-sample.

NumberFields.Description: Table: NumberFields. A finite extension K of Q. Discriminant, degree, class number, and signature are raw inputs — but IsTotallyReal, IsTotallyComplex, and SatisfiesGolodShafarevich are first-class calculated booleans on the same row, not separate property rows.

schema.name: SignatureRealEmbeddings
schema.Description: r_1: number of real embeddings.

schema.name: SignatureComplexEmbeddings
schema.Description: r_2: number of complex-conjugate pairs.

schema.name: AmbientLatticeDimension
schema.Description: The R-dimension of the Minkowski embedding image.

schema.name: IsPID
schema.Description: Ring of integers is a principal ideal domain iff class number is 1.

schema.name: SmallNormPrimeIdealCount
schema.Description: How many prime ideals fall below the small-norm threshold. Drives multiplicity in construction families.

schema.name: GolodShafarevichPassingCount
schema.Description: Aggregation count of GolodShafarevichCriteria rows for this field that satisfy the GS tower inequality. Used as the bridge from raw GS parameters to a per-field boolean.

schema.name: SatisfiesGolodShafarevich
schema.Description: TRUE if some GolodShafarevichCriteria row for this field passes the tower inequality. This is the gate that determines whether the field is a candidate algebraic source for many short vectors.

schema.name: IsAlgebraicSourceCandidate
schema.Description: TRUE when the field both satisfies GS and has at least one small-norm prime ideal — the two-condition algebraic precondition for producing a Minkowski lattice with many short vectors.

schema.name: FieldEmbeddingCount
schema.Description: How many field embeddings (real or complex) are explicitly loaded for this field. Sanity check: should equal SignatureRealEmbeddings + SignatureComplexEmbeddings.

schema.name: FieldEmbeddingCountMatchesSignature
schema.Description: Read-time integrity check: did we load every embedding the signature claims to exist?

PrimeIdeals.Description: Table: PrimeIdeals. A prime ideal in the ring of integers of a NumberField. Norm is raw; IsSmallNorm is a first-class calculated predicate that downstream aggregations consume.

schema.name: NormThreshold
schema.Description: Per-prime threshold for the 'small norm' classification.

schema.name: SplitsCompletely
schema.Description: Whether the prime in Q splits completely in O_K. Nullable: null when splitting behavior has been named but not yet computed.

data.GeneratorDescription: (5) — splitting behavior named but not yet computed
(1 - ζ_7) — the unique prime above 7, fully ramified (e=6, f=1)
one of two primes above 2; ord_7(2)=3 so f=3, e=1, two distinct primes of norm 8
one of six primes above 29; 29 ≡ 1 (mod 7) so 29 splits completely (e=1, f=1, g=6)

MinkowskiLattices.Description: Table: MinkowskiLattices. The image of O_K under the Minkowski embedding into R^(r_1 + 2*r_2). Carries Gram matrix as raw JSON (matrix algebra is not in scope for ERB formulas), but everything derivable from already-promoted quantities — Dimension, the determinant-equals-discriminant identity check — is a first-class field.

schema.name: DeterminantSquaredEqualsDiscriminant
schema.Description: Classical identity: covolume(O_K)^2 = |disc(K)|. Promoted as a first-class sanity check the model verifies on read.

schema.name: GramMatrixJSON
schema.Description: JSON-encoded Gram matrix. Matrix algebra is held opaque because ERB formulas are scalar; downstream substrates that support matrices may extend this.

schema.name: SourceFieldDegree
schema.Description: The degree [K:Q] of the source field, surfaced on the lattice for downstream chain checks.

schema.name: SourceFieldSatisfiesGolodShafarevich
schema.Description: The single most important upstream gate. TRUE means the field-side ingredient (GS tower) is satisfied — a necessary condition for the lattice to carry enough short vectors for a superlinear unit-distance construction.

schema.name: ProjectionCount
schema.Description: How many distinct planar projections have been registered from this lattice.

schema.name: HasAnyPlanarProjection
schema.Description: TRUE if any planar projection has been registered — the geometry-side leg of the bridge from lattice to plane.

schema.name: IsLoadBearingForUnitDistanceConstruction
schema.Description: TRUE when the lattice has a GS-satisfying source field, at least one short vector, and at least one planar projection — i.e. it is positioned to feed a ConstructionFamily that produces unit-distance witnesses.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Free-text annotation for this row.

data.Description: Minkowski lattice of Z[ζ_7] under the canonical embedding into R^6. Determinant sqrt(16807) ≈ 129.642 (= sqrt of |disc(K)|). The Gram matrix is stylized as the cyclotomic-like circulant pattern; the real Z[ζ_7] Gram on the power basis is integral but the entries are not as clean.

ShortVectors.Description: Table: ShortVectors. A vector in a MinkowskiLattice with bounded norm. NormSquared is raw (we are storing the lattice point's known squared length); IsShort is a calculated predicate against the lattice's threshold. The promotion: every individual short vector is a row — not a count, not a list — because each one carries a specific algebraic origin worth inspecting.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Free-text annotation for this row.

data.Description: Image of (1 - ζ_7) under Minkowski; just above the short-vector threshold (squared norm 6 > 4).

ConstructionFamilies.Description: Table: ConstructionFamilies. A parametrized family {P_n} of point sets. SourceNumberField and SourceLattice are optional — non-null only for algebraic constructions. Each row produces a sequence of ConstructionInstances which feed a GrowthSequence.

schema.name: SourceFieldSatisfiesGolodShafarevich
schema.Description: The GS-satisfaction of the source field, inherited via SourceNumberField. Null if the family is non-algebraic (no source field set).

schema.name: IsAlgebraicConstruction
schema.Description: TRUE iff the family's algebraic upstream is GS-passing AND its lattice has a projection + short vectors. Non-algebraic families (random points, ad-hoc geometric tricks) will be FALSE.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Free-text annotation for this row.

data.DescriptionOfNthMember: Project the first n short vectors of the Minkowski lattice of Q(sqrt(-3)) into R^2 via the embedding's first two real coordinates.

Project the n smallest elements of Z[ζ_7] under σ_1 into R^2. The n=8 instance is the heptagon-with-center; larger n loads additional non-unit lattice elements (sums and differences). Sawin-style higher n configurations involve restricting to elements with controlled multi-σ norms — not loaded here.
A purely geometric family (unit triangle, unit square, etc.) with no algebraic source. Used as a contrast — IsAlgebraicConstruction is FALSE for this family.
data.Description: Coverage row: a non-algebraic family. Its instances point at PointSets that aren't projections of any lattice.

ConstructionInstances.Description: Table: ConstructionInstances. The materialized P_n for a specific n. The PointSet it produces is a real row in PointSets — so all the geometry-layer aggregations apply to it without re-implementation. EdgeCount and DensityExponentEstimate are LOOKUPS, not duplicates.

schema.name: ParamNMatchesPointCount
schema.Description: Sanity check: did the n-th instance actually produce n points?

schema.name: FamilyIsAlgebraic
schema.Description: Surfaces the family-level algebraic-construction flag onto each instance so per-row checks can see it.

schema.name: IsExplicitSuperlinear
schema.Description: TRUE if this single finite instance already exhibits log(edges)/log(points) > 1 — a per-instance witness that the family is achieving superlinear edge growth at this n.

schema.name: IsAlgebraicSuperlinearWitness
schema.Description: A single instance that is both (a) coming from an algebraically valid family AND (b) finitely superlinear. This is the most direct per-row evidence we have that the algebra is producing the geometry.

GrowthSequences.Description: Table: GrowthSequences. The sequence (n, e(P_n)) realized by a ConstructionFamily, summarized as aggregations over its instances. MaxObservedDensityExponentEstimate is the strongest finite-sample witness the family currently provides — and is the value AsymptoticLowerBounds compares against to decide whether a claimed bound is finitely consistent.

AsymptoticFunctions.Description: Table: AsymptoticFunctions. Named asymptotic functions like U(n). DefinitionText is the human-readable definition; the function's content is fully specified by the AsymptoticLowerBounds and (eventually) AsymptoticUpperBounds that reference it.

schema.name: BestKnownLowerBoundExponent
schema.Description: ALL-TIME best lower-bound exponent across every loaded row, including retracted ones. Useful for forensic queries; usually NOT what you want for 'what does the rulebook currently believe'.

schema.name: BestKnownCurrentLowerBoundExponent
schema.Description: Bitemporally-filtered best lower-bound exponent: only counts bounds where IsCurrentlyValid=TRUE. This is what 'best known' actually means under the bitemporal discipline. Retracted overclaims, withdrawn preprints, and pending-evaluation rows are excluded.

schema.name: RetractedLowerBoundCount
schema.Description: How many lower-bound rows for this function have been retracted/superseded. Cumulative scar count.

schema.name: MaxOverclaimedExponent
schema.Description: Highest exponent that was once claimed but is no longer current. Forensic indicator of overclaim history.

data.DefinitionText: U(n) = max over P ⊂ R^2 with |P|=n of e(G(P)) — the maximum number of unit-distance pairs among n planar points.

AsymptoticLowerBounds.Description: Table: AsymptoticLowerBounds. A claim of the form 'AsymptoticFunction(n) ≥ Coefficient · n^Exponent for sufficiently large n'. Exponent is raw. IsSuperlinear, WitnessConsistent, and ExceedsTrivialLinearBound are all first-class calculated checks. WitnessConsistent compares the claimed exponent against MaxObservedDensityExponentEstimate from the supporting GrowthSequence — a finite-sample sanity check that doesn't replace the proof but does flag inconsistency.

schema.name: IsExplicit
schema.Description: True if the exponent is named numerically (e.g. 1.014), false if only existential ('some ε > 0').

schema.name: WitnessConsistent
schema.Description: Finite-sample sanity check: do the family's observed instances achieve at least the claimed exponent? FALSE means either more instances are needed or the claim is wrong for this family.

schema.name: WitnessSourceFamily
schema.Description: Surfaces the ConstructionFamily backing this bound's witness, two FK hops upstream.

schema.name: WitnessFamilyIsAlgebraic
schema.Description: TRUE iff the family providing this bound's finite witness is an algebraic construction with GS-passing source field. Distinguishes 'algebraically-anchored bound' from 'bound with a generic geometric witness'.

schema.name: IsAlgebraicallyAnchored
schema.Description: TRUE when (a) the finite witness is consistent with the claimed exponent, (b) the witness family is algebraic-source, and (c) the claim itself is superlinear. This is the cleanest single-cell answer to 'does this bound carry the algebraic ladder all the way through?'

schema.name: AllObligationsSatisfied
schema.Description: TRUE iff every proof obligation referencing this bound is satisfied. FALSE means at least one obligation is unsatisfied or unevaluated.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: ISO date when this claim first entered valid-time (typically the publication date of the paper that asserts it).

schema.name: ValidTo
schema.Description: ISO date when this claim left valid-time (retraction, withdrawal). Null = still valid in the real world.

schema.name: TxFrom
schema.Description: ISO date when this row was first recorded in the rulebook (transaction-time audit start).

schema.name: TxTo
schema.Description: ISO date when this row was logically removed from the rulebook. Null = still present in current transaction.

schema.name: IsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: Bitemporal current-validity flag. TRUE = claim is still considered valid; FALSE = superseded/withdrawn; null = not yet evaluated. Drives every 'current best known' aggregation in the asymptotic layer.

schema.name: ProofPathway
schema.Description: How the bound is proven. One of: 'algebraic-tower' (Sawin-style, via class field towers + Minkowski lattices), 'combinatorial-pigeonhole' (Erdős 1946, via integer-grid + divisor-function argument), 'crossing-number' (via crossing-number inequality), 'incidence-counting' (via point-line incidence bounds), 'witness-only' (finite-sample claim with no asymptotic proof), 'other'.

schema.name: IsAuditableViaItsPathway
schema.Description: Pathway-appropriate audit gate. Algebraic-tower proofs need IsAlgebraicallyAnchored; combinatorial-pigeonhole proofs need obligations satisfied + finite witness consistent; witness-only claims just need WitnessConsistent. Fixes the bias of the older AlgebraicChainClosed gate, which would mis-rule Erdős's pigeonhole proof as 'not closed' simply because it doesn't use class field towers.

data.StatementText: U(n) ≥ n^{1+c/log log n} (Erdős 1946). Proven via integer-grid construction: take the √n × √n grid Z² ∩ [0,√n]², scale so a frequent distance equals 1, then count: by Landau's theorem on sums of two squares, certain integers k have r₂(k) representations growing like (log k)^{log 2 - 1}, and these representations yield multi-pair unit distances after scaling.

U(n) > n^{1.014} for arbitrarily large n. Sawin's explicit refinement (2026-05-20) of OpenAI's autonomous reasoning-model discovery (announced same day). Construction uses a cyclotomic field K = Q(ζ_p) for chosen prime p satisfying the Golod–Shafarevich criterion, projects O_K's Minkowski lattice into R² via the first complex embedding σ_1, and counts unit-length differences among short vectors. The infinite class field tower from G–S provides the small-norm-prime density that makes the construction superlinear.
U(n) ≥ n^{0.9} witnessed by the heptagon-with-center configuration (n=8, e=7 ⇒ exponent ≈ 0.937). A deliberately under-claimed exponent so WitnessConsistent resolves TRUE on the loaded instance.
data.SourceCitation: computed from loaded data, no external citation

data.StatementText: Hypothetical: U(n) > n^{1.5} — a stylized overclaim that was once asserted and later retracted. Coverage row demonstrating valid-time retraction.
Coverage row: a bound entered into the rulebook but not yet evaluated for current validity. IsCurrentlyValid=null.

Theorems.Description: Table: Theorems. Fixed-point anchors. A theorem refers to an AsymptoticFunction and (when anchoring a bound) a specific AsymptoticLowerBound. AnchorMatchesBoundExponent is a calculated check that the anchor's named exponent equals the referenced bound's exponent — the kind of paperwork mistake that should be caught at read-time, not in review.

schema.name: ClaimedExponent
schema.Description: The exponent the theorem statement claims, recorded redundantly so the read-time check below can compare it against the bound's recorded exponent.

schema.name: AnchorMatchesBoundExponent
schema.Description: Read-time integrity check that the theorem and its anchored bound agree on the exponent.

schema.name: AnchoredBoundWitnessConsistent
schema.Description: Surfaces the bound-level finite-witness consistency onto the theorem row.

schema.name: AnchoredBoundIsAlgebraicallyAnchored
schema.Description: Surfaces the bound-level algebraic-anchoring onto the theorem row.

schema.name: AlgebraicChainClosed
schema.Description: THE ladder check. TRUE iff (a) the theorem's claimed exponent matches the anchored bound, (b) the bound is superlinear, (c) the finite witness is consistent with the claim, (d) the witness family is algebraic with GS-passing source. When this is TRUE for a row, the entire chain from number field to theorem is closed in the rulebook.

schema.name: AppliesToFunction
schema.Description: Human-readable name of the function the theorem talks about. Multi-anchor support — different theorems anchor different functions in the same neighborhood.

schema.name: IsUnitDistanceTheorem
schema.Description: TRUE if this theorem is about U(n). Other anchors in this neighborhood (Szemerédi–Trotter, etc.) are about different functions.

schema.name: AnchoredBoundAllObligationsSatisfied
schema.Description: Surfaces the anchored bound's full-obligation status onto the theorem row.

schema.name: FullyAuditedAndClosed
schema.Description: The final, fully-paranoid theorem-level gate: the algebraic chain is closed AND every proof obligation against the anchored bound is satisfied. TRUE means the theorem row is end-to-end witnessed in the rulebook with no open obligations.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: ISO date this theorem was first established (proof publication date for proven; statement date for unproven anchors).

schema.name: IsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: TRUE for current valid theorems; FALSE for retracted/disproven; null for unevaluated.

schema.name: AnchoredBoundIsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: Surfaces the anchored bound's current-validity onto the theorem. If the anchored bound got retracted, this goes FALSE — and the theorem either needs re-anchoring or also needs retraction.

schema.name: IsHistoricallyAnchored
schema.Description: TRUE iff both the theorem and its anchored bound are currently valid. Bitemporally-aware version of the older AlgebraicChainClosed gate — catches the case where a theorem's anchored bound has been retracted.

schema.name: AnchoredBoundIsAuditableViaItsPathway
schema.Description: Surfaces the anchored bound's pathway-appropriate audit gate onto the theorem.

schema.name: IsAuditedAndClosed
schema.Description: The pathway-aware replacement for FullyAuditedAndClosed. TRUE iff the theorem is bitemporally valid, its anchored bound is bitemporally valid, AND the anchored bound passes its OWN pathway's audit gate (algebraic chain for Sawin-style; obligations+witness for Erdős-style pigeonhole). Erdős's theorem closes here even though the older algebraic-only gate said it couldn't.

data.StatementText: U(n) > n^{1.014} for arbitrarily large n. First open problem central to a subfield of mathematics solved autonomously by AI — OpenAI's internal reasoning model produced the proof (inexplicit superlinear exponent) on 2026-05-20; Sawin's companion paper made the exponent explicit at 1.014 the same day. The model was general-purpose: not domain-trained, not scaffolded for proof search, not targeted at the unit-distance problem. Disproves Erdős 1946's polynomial-improvement conjecture (the long-held belief that square-grid constructions were optimal).
U(n) ≥ n^{1+c/log log n} (Erdős 1946).
The number of point-line incidences among n points and m lines in R^2 is O((nm)^{2/3} + n + m).
U(n) = O(n^{4/3}) — the classical upper bound that the lower-bound program is closing in on.
Reserved row for a future tighter lower bound — schema-only, not yet proven.
Hypothetical: a theorem that was claimed, briefly anchored, then retracted when its proof was found flawed.

Metrics.Description: Table: Metrics. First-class distance functions on a space. The Euclidean metric on R^2, the Gram-matrix-induced metric on a lattice, and non-metric similarity functions all live here as named rows. Downstream tables that 'use a distance' should point at a Metrics row rather than hard-coding the formula.

schema.name: MetricKind
schema.Description: One of: euclidean, lattice-gram, taxicab, cosine-similarity, experimental.

schema.name: SatisfiesTriangleInequality
schema.Description: Nullable: TRUE for genuine metrics, FALSE for similarity-only functions, null for unevaluated experimental rows.

data.Description: Standard 2D Euclidean distance — the metric used by every PointPair row.

data.DefinitionText: d(v,w)^2 = (v-w)^T G (v-w) for Gram matrix G of the lattice
data.Description: The intrinsic lattice metric — equals the Euclidean metric only when the embedding is isometric.
L1 metric — included to show the Metrics table is general.
Not a true metric — coverage row for SatisfiesTriangleInequality=false.
Unevaluated — coverage row for SatisfiesTriangleInequality=null.

FieldEmbeddings.Description: Table: FieldEmbeddings. The individual embeddings σ_i: K → R or σ_j: K → C that constitute the Minkowski embedding. One row per σ. Aggregation by EmbeddingType reproduces the signature (r_1, r_2) at read time.

schema.name: EmbeddingType
schema.Description: One of: 'real' or 'complex'.

schema.name: IndexInSignature
schema.Description: 1-based index within the signature.

schema.name: TargetSpaceDim
schema.Description: 1 for real, 2 for complex.

schema.name: HasConjugatePairInField
schema.Description: Null for real embeddings (concept doesn't apply); TRUE for complex embeddings whose conjugate is in K; FALSE for hypothetical degenerate complex embeddings without their partner.

data.Description: The single complex embedding pair of Q(sqrt(-3)).
Coverage row: real embedding — conjugate-pair concept doesn't apply, so HasConjugatePairInField is null.
Coverage row: hypothetical complex embedding whose conjugate is NOT in K — for nullable-bool data coverage.

MinkowskiEmbeddings.Description: Table: MinkowskiEmbeddings. The canonical embedding σ = (σ_1, …, σ_d): K → R^d as a first-class named map. Each row binds one NumberField to (at most) one TargetLattice it produces.

schema.name: TargetLattice
schema.Description: Nullable: the field may have a defined embedding map even if the materialised lattice row isn't loaded yet.

schema.name: IsCanonical
schema.Description: TRUE if this is the canonical Minkowski embedding.

schema.name: PreservesAdditiveStructure
schema.Description: Nullable: TRUE for canonical embeddings, FALSE for non-additive maps, null for unevaluated experimental rows.

data.Description: Canonical Minkowski embedding of Q(sqrt(-3)) into R^2.
Canonical embedding of Q(ζ_7) into R^6 (signature (0,3) means 3 complex pairs ⇒ ambient R^6).
Coverage row: hypothetical non-additive map.
Coverage row: unevaluated additivity.

GramMatrices.Description: Table: GramMatrices. The Gram matrix G_ij = <e_i, e_j> for a chosen basis of a MinkowskiLattice. Matrix entries stay in MatrixJSON (ERB scalar formulas can't operate on matrices), but every scalar property derivable from G — symmetric, positive-definite, diagonal, rank-deficient — is a first-class field.

schema.name: EncodesValidLatticeMetric
schema.Description: TRUE iff G is symmetric and positive-definite — the algebraic conditions for G to induce a genuine metric.

data.Description: Coverage row: singular Gram-like matrix.
Coverage row: unverified.
Coverage row: non-diagonal but still positive-definite.
Stylized circulant-pattern Gram for Z[ζ_7]; det = 16807 = |disc(K)|. Real entries on the power basis of Z[ζ_7] are integer but messier — this entry is a clean stand-in.

PlanarProjections.Description: Table: PlanarProjections. The map π: lattice → R^2 that turns short lattice vectors into points in the plane. THE critical bridge from algebra to geometry. ProjectionKind tags the kind of map; downstream ProjectedShortVectors.ProjectsToUnitDistanceVector depends on this map's behaviour.

schema.name: ProjectionKind
schema.Description: One of: 'first-two-coords', 'rotation-scale', 'rational-affine', 'isometric-embedding'.

schema.name: PreservesUnitDistance
schema.Description: TRUE if vectors of squared-norm 1 in the lattice map to unit-distance vectors in R^2.

schema.name: UnitDistanceVectorCount
schema.Description: How many projected short vectors land at squared-norm 1 — i.e. survive as unit-distance witnesses. The geometric yield of the algebra-to-plane bridge.

schema.name: UnitDistanceVectorYield
schema.Description: Fraction of projected short vectors that are unit-distance witnesses.

data.Description: Coverage row: scaling by 2 destroys unit-distance preservation.
Coverage row: properties not yet evaluated.

Pick out the first complex embedding σ_1: ζ_7 ↦ e^(2πi/7) and read off (Re, Im) ∈ R^2. NOT isometric (the R^2 image is a 2D shadow of a 6D lattice); whether it preserves unit distance depends on which lattice points get sampled — captured row-by-row in ProjectedShortVectors.

data.DisplayName: Scaled σ1 Projection of Lat Q-zeta-7
data.Description: σ1 followed by scaling by 1/sqrt(3) so that the powers of ζ_7 (which have squared L2-norm 3 in R^6) land near the unit circle in R^2.

ProjectedShortVectors.Description: Table: ProjectedShortVectors. Each row is one ShortVector under one PlanarProjection — the point in R^2 the algebraic vector becomes. ProjectsToUnitDistanceVector is THE planar-unit target equation <π(v), π(v)> = 1 — the equation Sawin's construction is solving. Every TRUE row is one solution, contributing one unit-distance edge.

schema.name: ProjectedNormSquared
schema.Description: <π(v), π(v)> — the LHS of the planar-unit target equation.

schema.name: ProjectsToUnitDistanceVector
schema.Description: THE planar-unit target equation: <π(v), π(v)> = 1. This is the equation Sawin's construction is solving — every TRUE row is one solution.

schema.name: IsValidWitness
schema.Description: The two-sided certificate: source vector is short (algebraic side) AND projection has squared norm 1 (geometric side).

schema.name: NormPreservedUnderProjection
schema.Description: TRUE if the projection preserved the squared norm on this vector — locally isometric.

data.Description: v1 under rotation-and-scale: squared norm becomes ≈4, no longer a unit-distance witness.
1 under σ_1: (1,0). Squared norm 1 — solves the target equation.
ζ_7 under σ_1: (cos(2π/7), sin(2π/7)). On the unit circle — solves the target equation.
ζ_7^2 under σ_1: (cos(4π/7), sin(4π/7)).
ζ_7^3 under σ_1: (cos(6π/7), sin(6π/7)).
(1 - ζ_7) under σ_1: squared norm = 2 - 2cos(2π/7) ≈ 0.7530, near but not at the unit circle. Does NOT solve the target equation.
2·1 under σ_1: distance 2 from origin. Source vector is long; projection is also long. Both predicates FALSE.

GolodShafarevichCriteria.Description: Table: GolodShafarevichCriteria. The Golod–Shafarevich tower inequality, evaluated per number field. r > d^2/4 (d = minimal-generator count, r = relation count) implies an infinite class field tower — the algebraic hook used by unit-distance constructions to get many small-norm primes.

schema.name: MinimalGeneratorCount_d
schema.Description: The 'd' in the GS inequality.

schema.name: RelationCount_r
schema.Description: The 'r' in the GS inequality.

schema.name: CriterionThreshold
schema.Description: d^2/4. The bound that r must exceed.

schema.name: SatisfiesGolodShafarevichTower
schema.Description: TRUE iff r > d^2/4 — the GS inequality is satisfied and K has an infinite class field tower.

schema.name: PassesCriterion
schema.Description: Curator override (nullable). Null means 'use calculated SatisfiesGolodShafarevichTower'.

data.Description: Q(sqrt(-3)): d=2, r=1, threshold=1; 1 is not > 1 — fails GS.
Q(ζ_7) demonstration values: d=3, r=5, threshold=2.25; 5>2.25 — satisfies GS. Demonstration only; not a rigorous certificate.
Coverage row: PassesCriterion unevaluated.

SemanticBridges.Description: Table: SemanticBridges. Each row is one named bridge between two domain neighborhoods — the meta-glue between regions of the model. Routing across the model goes through these bridges.

schema.name: BridgeKind
schema.Description: One of: embedding, projection, induction, aggregation, anchor, analogy.

schema.name: IsLoadBearing
schema.Description: TRUE if removing the bridge breaks the chain to the theorem; FALSE for analogy-only; null when unclassified.

schema.name: IsClosed
schema.Description: TRUE if every row in FromConceptTable has a witness in ToConceptTable; null when not checked.

data.Description: The unit-distance predicate over PointPairs induces the edge set of the UnitDistanceGraph.
Canonical Minkowski embedding O_K ↪ R^d. Both loaded fields (Q(sqrt(-3)) → lat-q-sqrt-neg3, Q(ζ_7) → lat-q-zeta-7) have witnesses.
Projection π: lattice → R^2. The critical algebraic-to-geometric bridge.
Finite-n edge counts aggregate into a growth sequence.
A theorem anchors a specific lower bound.
Conceptual analogy. Not load-bearing.
Coverage row: hypothetical reverse bridge.
Coverage row: bridge whose source has rows without corresponding target witnesses (q-zeta-7's MinkowskiEmbedding has no PlanarProjection).

SemanticRoutes.Description: Table: SemanticRoutes. Named end-to-end paths through the model. The 'full semantic spine' is the route NumberFields → Theorems through every bridge.

schema.name: AllStepsValidated
schema.Description: TRUE iff every step on the route has IsValidated=TRUE.

data.Description: Points → PointSets → PointPairs → UnitDistanceGraphs.
NumberFields → FieldEmbeddings → MinkowskiEmbeddings → MinkowskiLattices → PlanarProjections.
ConstructionInstances → GrowthSequences → AsymptoticLowerBounds.
End-to-end inference chain: algebra → lattice → projection → planar geometry → growth → bound → theorem.
Coverage row: hypothetical bypass route, not yet classified.

SemanticRouteSteps.Description: Table: SemanticRouteSteps. The ordered inference steps that make up a SemanticRoute. Each step optionally invokes a SemanticBridge.

schema.name: BridgeUsed
schema.Description: Null when this step is an intra-domain FK hop; set when crossing domains.

schema.name: IsValidated
schema.Description: Curator-set: TRUE if verified, FALSE if known broken, null if not yet evaluated.

data.Description: Coverage row: not yet validated.
Coverage row: marked invalid — shortcut bypasses load-bearing bridges.

SourceReferences.Description: Table: SourceReferences. The bibliography. Every lemma, theorem, and bound points at exactly one SourceReference row.

schema.name: OutboundCitationCount
schema.Description: How many other sources this one cites.

schema.name: InboundCitationCount
schema.Description: How many other sources cite this one — a crude influence metric.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: ISO publication date — the moment this source becomes citable.

schema.name: ValidTo
schema.Description: ISO retraction date (if any). Null = still in good standing.

schema.name: IsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: TRUE if the source is still in good standing; FALSE if retracted; null if standing not yet evaluated.

data.FullCitation: Sawin, W. (2026). 'An explicit lower bound for the unit distance problem.' arXiv:2605.20579 — companion paper to OpenAI's May 20, 2026 autonomous-AI announcement; makes the OpenAI proof's inexplicit exponent explicit at n^{1.014} using cyclotomic fields + Golod–Shafarevich tower + Minkowski lattice projection.

OpenAI (2026-05-20). 'An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry.' Official announcement at openai.com/index/model-disproves-discrete-geometry-conjecture/. First fully autonomous proof of a central open problem in mathematics: U(n) grows superlinearly with exponent > 1 (inexplicit), refuting the long-held belief that square-grid constructions are optimal. Produced by a general-purpose internal reasoning model — not domain-trained, not scaffolded for proof search, not targeted at the unit-distance problem.
data.Description: The primary discovery announcement. Sawin's companion paper (src-sawin-2026) makes the exponent explicit; this announcement is the first-mover record.

data.FullCitation: Erdős, P. 'On sets of distances of n points.' American Mathematical Monthly 53 (1946) 248-250.
Szemerédi, E. and Trotter, W. T. 'Extremal problems in discrete geometry.' Combinatorica 3 (1983) 381-392.
Spencer, J., Szemerédi, E., and Trotter, W. T. 'Unit distances in the Euclidean plane.' Graph Theory and Combinatorics (1984) 293-303.
Golod, E. S. and Shafarevich, I. R. 'On the class field tower.' Izv. Akad. Nauk SSSR Ser. Mat. 28 (1964) 261-272.

data.Description: Coverage row: nullable booleans null; ValidFrom is a placeholder since folklore predates exact dating.
Coverage row: retracted/withdrawn source. IsCurrentlyValid=false.

Lemmas.Description: Table: Lemmas. Extracted named statements from source references. FeedsObjectTable + FeedsObjectId point at the row this lemma underwrites.

schema.name: IsLoaded
schema.Description: TRUE if the lemma's content has been turned into data; FALSE if known but not loaded; null if unevaluated.

schema.name: ObligationCount
schema.Description: How many bounds depend on this lemma.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: ISO date this lemma was first asserted (inherits from SourceReference).

schema.name: IsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: TRUE if the lemma is still considered valid; FALSE if refuted/retracted; null if unevaluated.

data.Label: Short-vector counting for GS fields
data.StatementText: For number fields satisfying Golod–Shafarevich with controlled discriminant growth, the count of short vectors of squared norm at most R in the Minkowski lattice is at least c·R^{1+δ} for some explicit δ>0.

data.Label: Edge density margin over linear
data.StatementText: When the lattice is projected via the canonical map, the resulting point set has edge count exceeding n^{1.014} for arbitrarily large n.

data.Label: Explicit n^1.014 anchor
data.StatementText: There exists an explicit construction family (anchored on Q(ζ_p) with appropriate p) realizing the n^{1.014} lower bound.

data.Label: Spencer 1984 auxiliary count
data.StatementText: Auxiliary edge-count bound used in classical incidence proofs.
data.Description: Named but not extracted as data yet; coverage row for IsCurrentlyValid=null.

data.Label: Future improvement lemma slot
data.StatementText: Reserved row for a forthcoming improvement.
data.Description: Inherits retraction from src-experimental-preprint.

data.Label: Erdős 1946 main theorem
data.StatementText: Main theorem of Erdős 1946: U(n) ≥ c · n^{1 + c'/log log n} for some absolute constants c, c' > 0. Proof outline: take the integer grid Z² ∩ [0, √n]² (which has n points), compute all pairwise distances-squared, observe that these are sums of two squares, then apply Landau-style multiplicative bounds on r₂(k) (the number of representations of k as a²+b²) to extract a frequent distance whose multiplicity is Ω(n / (log n)^{1 - log 2}). Scaling the grid so that frequent distance becomes 1 yields the claimed unit-distance count.
data.Description: Updated in Loop 5 with real proof content. IsLoadBearing flipped from false to true; this IS what supports the Erdős bound.

data.Label: Integer-grid pigeonhole construction
data.StatementText: Let G_n = Z² ∩ [0, √n]² be the √n × √n integer-grid point set; |G_n| ≈ n. For any pair (p, q) ∈ G_n × G_n, |p - q|² ∈ Z. The multiset of pairwise squared distances is concentrated: the integer k = a² + b² appears Θ(n · r₂(k)/k) times among pairs at squared-distance k (where r₂ counts representations). For k = m² · ∏ p_i with p_i ≡ 1 (mod 4), r₂(k) grows; choosing k with r₂(k) = Ω((log n)^{log 2}) gives a single squared-distance achieved Ω(n^{1+c/loglogn}) times.
data.Description: NEW in Loop 5. The 'grid → distance histogram' step of Erdős's proof. The integer-distance restriction is what makes pigeonhole work — distances on a real-coordinate point set don't concentrate, integer-grid distances do.

data.Label: Sum-of-two-squares representation count r₂(k)
data.StatementText: Number-theoretic fact (Landau, Hardy–Wright): the representation function r₂(k) = #{(a,b) ∈ Z² : a²+b² = k} is multiplicative; for k = 2^a · ∏ p_i^{α_i} · ∏ q_j^{β_j} with p_i ≡ 1 (mod 4) and q_j ≡ 3 (mod 4), r₂(k) = 4 · ∏(α_i+1) if all β_j even, else 0. For k = product of t primes ≡ 1 (mod 4), r₂(k) = 4 · 2^t. Choosing k with t ~ log log n gives r₂(k) ~ (log n)^{log 2}, the multiplicity needed for Erdős's bound.
data.Description: NEW in Loop 5. The number-theoretic supporting lemma — Erdős's bound rides on this multiplicative structure of r₂(k). Cited in 1946 to Landau; FeedsObject is the grid-construction lemma above (lemma chain: divisor-function → grid-construction → main-theorem).

data.Label: Crossing number inequality (ACNS 1982)
data.StatementText: Ajtai–Chvátal–Newborn–Szemerédi 1982: any drawing of a graph G with n vertices and m ≥ 4n edges in the plane has at least c · m³/n² crossings (for some absolute constant c > 0). The Spencer–Szemerédi–Trotter unit-distance upper bound U(n) = O(n^{4/3}) follows from applying this to the unit-distance graph drawn with arcs.
data.Description: NEW in Loop 5. The crossing-number-inequality lemma that powers the upper-bound side of the sandwich on U(n). Real content from training, not placeholder.

data.Label: Historical context (background, not load-bearing)
data.StatementText: Background-only: Erdős conjectured in conversation that U(n) might be expressible as a polylogarithmic factor times n. This is context, not a step in any proof. Coverage row for IsLoadBearing=FALSE.
data.Description: Coverage row for IsLoadBearing=FALSE — historical context that is loaded but doesn't underwrite any specific bound.

MirrorContract.Description: Table: MirrorContract. The five-rule contract from THE_PLAN.md §3.1 governing how the rulebook mirrors the math. Each rule is a row so agents and substrates can quote it back rather than reading the markdown.

schema.name: RuleKind
schema.Description: One of: load, describe, derive, gate, reject.

schema.name: IsBindingOnAgents
schema.Description: TRUE if agents must follow this rule; FALSE if deprecated; null if under discussion.

data.RuleStatement: Every named mathematical object in the source papers must appear as a row in the rulebook before the rulebook can claim to mirror the result.
The rulebook describes what the paper proves; it does not attempt to derive new mathematical content. Calculated fields surface relationships already present in the data.
Modeler-added objects are allowed only with OriginatedHere=true. Speculative intermediates that are not in the source are rejected.
Columns that track the modeler's certainty (GapStatus, candidate, partial_candidate, …) are rejected at the storage layer. Curation notes live in commit messages, not in row data.
A rulebook is only valid when it compiles to at least two structurally-different substrates and produces field-for-field identical outputs against the same input data, gated by an answer-key.
Hypothetical: allow non-source rows when prefixed `hypothetical-`. Under discussion.
data.Description: Coverage row: rule not yet binding.

data.RuleStatement: Earlier draft superseded by the formal aggregation field type.
data.Description: Coverage row: deprecated rule retained for audit.

Conjectures.Description: Table: Conjectures. Open and resolved conjectures about an AsymptoticFunction — distinct from Theorems (which are proved) because a conjecture is named, has a target exponent, and carries a resolution status that may change over time. Erdős's polynomial-improvement conjecture sits here; Sawin's resolved-as-proved conjecture-then-theorem is cross-referenced.

schema.name: ConjecturedExponent
schema.Description: The exponent the conjecture asserts. Null when the conjecture is non-exponent (e.g., a structural claim).

schema.name: IsResolved
schema.Description: TRUE if proved/disproved; FALSE if explicitly still open; null when status hasn't been classified.

schema.name: ResolvedAs
schema.Description: One of: 'proved', 'disproved', 'withdrawn'. Null when not resolved.

schema.name: RelatedTheorem
schema.Description: If this conjecture resolved into a theorem, the theorem row is here.

schema.name: IsStillOpen
schema.Description: TRUE only when IsResolved is explicitly FALSE. Null IsResolved propagates to non-TRUE.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: ISO date this conjecture was first proposed.

schema.name: ValidTo
schema.Description: ISO date this conjecture left open status (resolved or withdrawn). Null = still open.

schema.name: IsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: TRUE iff the conjecture is still an OPEN question. Resolved or withdrawn ⇒ FALSE. Null = unevaluated. NOTE: this is bitemporal-status, NOT the truth of the underlying proposition — a conjecture-that-became-a-theorem has IsCurrentlyValid=FALSE here but the proposition itself is now TRUE in Theorems.

data.StatementText: U(n) = O(n^{1+ε}) for every ε > 0 — i.e., the unit-distance count is no more than slightly-superlinear.
data.Description: The most famous open question about U(n). Still open as of every loaded source.

data.StatementText: U(n) ≤ c · n^{4/3} — that the Spencer–Szemerédi–Trotter upper bound is tight up to constants.
data.Description: Folklore-strength: would mean ST is the right shape.

data.DisplayName: Polynomial superlinear improvement conjecture (resolved by OpenAI + Sawin 2026)
data.StatementText: Pre-2026 conjecture: there exists an explicit construction giving U(n) > n^c for some c > 1, for arbitrarily large n. Resolved 2026-05-20 by OpenAI's autonomous reasoning model (inexplicit c > 1) + Sawin's same-day explicit companion paper (c = 1.014 via cyclotomic + Golod–Shafarevich).
data.Description: Open for 80 years; resolved 2026-05-20. ValidTo marks the resolution moment. The proposition itself is now TRUE as a theorem (RelatedTheorem). The pre-resolution proposer is Erdős (1946 framing); the resolver is OpenAI's autonomous model.

data.StatementText: Any future improvement beyond n^1.014 — a structural placeholder.
data.Description: Coverage row: IsResolved=null, IsCurrentlyValid=null.

data.StatementText: A hypothetical conjecture proposed and later withdrawn by its author — for ResolvedAs='withdrawn' coverage.
data.Description: Coverage row. ValidTo marks the withdrawal date.

ProofObligations.Description: Table: ProofObligations. Bidirectional links between AsymptoticLowerBounds and the Lemmas that must be loaded for the bound to be derivable. Each row says 'bound X depends on lemma Y'. Aggregations on the bound side surface how many obligations are open vs satisfied.

schema.name: ObligationKind
schema.Description: One of: 'necessary', 'sufficient', 'auxiliary'.

schema.name: IsSatisfied
schema.Description: TRUE if the curator has confirmed the lemma justifies its part of the bound; FALSE if known gap; null if unevaluated.

schema.name: IsCurrentlyOpen
schema.Description: TRUE only if explicitly FALSE — i.e. a known gap.

data.Description: Open obligation: Spencer auxiliary lemma is named but not yet loaded.
Coverage row: IsSatisfied=null, IsCurrentlyValid=false (the required lemma was retracted).
Coverage row: IsCurrentlyValid=null (newly added, not yet curator-validated).
NEW in Loop 5. Erdős's bound depends on the integer-grid construction lemma being loaded — which it now is.
NEW in Loop 5. Erdős's bound also depends on the r₂(k) divisor-function structure lemma.

CitationLinks.Description: Table: CitationLinks. Directed graph of citations and dependencies between SourceReferences. CitationKind classifies whether the citing source extends, improves, depends on, or merely cites the cited source.

schema.name: CitationKind
schema.Description: One of: 'extends', 'improves', 'cites-for-context', 'depends-on', 'disagrees-with'.

schema.name: IsLoadBearing
schema.Description: TRUE if removing the cited source breaks the citing source's proof; FALSE for context-only; null when unclassified.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: ISO date this citation became real (matches the citing source's publication date).

schema.name: IsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: TRUE iff both the citing and cited sources are themselves still currently valid; FALSE if either has been retracted; null if unevaluated.

data.Description: Sawin's n^1.014 explicit-exponent bound improves on Erdős's existential n^{1+c/loglogn}.
Sawin's construction depends on number fields satisfying the Golod–Shafarevich criterion.
Szemerédi–Trotter is the upper-bound side; Sawin cites it for context, not as a dependency.
Spencer–Szemerédi–Trotter's n^{4/3} upper bound — Sawin's lower bound is far below this ceiling.
Spencer-S-T extends the original ST incidence bound to specifically unit distances.
Coverage row: IsLoadBearing=null, IsCurrentlyValid=null.
Coverage row: citing source was retracted, so the citation is no longer current.
Sawin's companion paper makes the OpenAI model's inexplicit superlinear exponent explicit at n^{1.014}. Companion paper; same publication day.
OpenAI's autonomous proof refutes the central conjecture from Erdős 1946 that square-grid constructions are optimal.
The reasoning model autonomously connected the unit-distance problem to algebraic number theory via the Golod–Shafarevich criterion — making the cited dependency explicit.

AnswerKey.Description: Table: AnswerKey. The §14 substrate-equivalence gate from THE_PLAN.md. Each row pins ONE cell (TargetTable + TargetRowId + TargetField) to its expected value. Every conformance-tested substrate (Postgres, Python, OWL, …) must reproduce these values; any mismatch with GateLevel='blocking' fails the build.

schema.name: DataType
schema.Description: One of: 'string', 'number', 'boolean'.

schema.name: Tolerance
schema.Description: Numeric tolerance for floating-point comparisons. Null for exact-match.

schema.name: GateLevel
schema.Description: One of: 'blocking', 'warning', 'info'.

schema.name: IsCurrentlyMatched
schema.Description: TRUE if the most recent substrate run matched the expected value; FALSE if it didn't; null if not yet run.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: ISO date when this expected-value pin was first asserted.

schema.name: ValidTo
schema.Description: ISO date when this pin was retired/changed.

schema.name: IsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: TRUE if the pin is still the active expected value; FALSE if superseded by a newer pin; null if unevaluated.

data.Description: Triangle A↔B is a unit-distance pair: ΔX²+ΔY² = 1.
Diagonal of the unit square: sqrt(2), so squared = 2.
7 spoke pairs (center→each vertex); chord pairs are not unit.
Q(sqrt(-3)) does NOT satisfy GS (d=2, r=1, threshold=1, 1 is not > 1).
Q(ζ_7) demonstration values satisfy GS.
Norms ≤ 30 in the loaded Q(ζ_7) primes: 7, 8, 8, 29 → 4 primes.
Classical identity det² = |disc(K)|: 129.642² ≈ 16807 = |disc(Q(ζ_7))|.

Honest finding: the n^1.014 bound is NOT finite-sample witnessed by the loaded sqrt-lattice instances (which max out at exponent 1). Marked 'info' rather than 'blocking' because this reflects what's loaded, not a substrate bug.
The deliberately-under-claimed Q(ζ_7) bound (exponent 0.9) IS witnessed by the heptagon-with-center instance (log7/log8 ≈ 0.937).
Honest: the Sawin theorem row's chain is NOT closed because the Sawin bound's WitnessConsistent is FALSE under the loaded data.
Coverage row: IsCurrentlyMatched=null and IsCurrentlyValid=null.
Coverage row: this expected-value pin was retired (superseded by a later one); IsCurrentlyValid=false.

TemporalSnapshots.Description: Table: TemporalSnapshots. Named moments in the historical progression of the unit-distance problem. Each row pins a calendar date with a label so chronology becomes a first-class entity. Aggregations against bitemporal columns elsewhere in the rulebook can reference these moments by name instead of by raw ISO string.

schema.name: SnapshotDate
schema.Description: ISO date for the moment this snapshot captures.

schema.name: IsHistorical
schema.Description: TRUE for past moments; FALSE for the current moment; null for future-projected moments.

schema.name: AnchoringSourceReference
schema.Description: The source whose publication date pins this moment, when applicable.

schema.name: ValidLowerBoundCountAtThisMoment
schema.Description: How many AsymptoticLowerBound rows were considered valid at this moment, per the bitemporal columns.

schema.name: BestKnownLowerBoundExponentAtThisMoment
schema.Description: Date-only as-of rollup: MAX exponent of bounds bitemporally valid at this snapshot, including unevaluated/pending rows. Use this for forensic 'what would have looked best at this moment'.

schema.name: CuratorConfirmedBestKnownLowerBoundExponentAtThisMoment
schema.Description: Stricter as-of rollup: MAX exponent of bounds BOTH bitemporally valid at this snapshot AND curator-confirmed (IsCurrentlyValid=TRUE on the bound itself). This is what the rulebook actually believed at this moment — excludes pending/unevaluated rows that just happen to be in their valid-time interval.

schema.name: PendingButValidByDateCount
schema.Description: Rows that are bitemporally valid here but the curator hasn't ruled on yet — i.e., the rulebook's open epistemic questions at this snapshot.

schema.name: BoundsValidatedOrRetractedThisMoment
schema.Description: Total junction rows for this snapshot — should equal the count of all AsymptoticLowerBound rows (a closure check).

data.Label: Erdős 1946 publication
data.Description: Erdős's original 'On sets of distances of n points' establishes U(n) ≥ n^{1+c/loglogn}. The starting line of the lower-bound program.

data.Label: Szemerédi–Trotter 1983
data.Description: Szemerédi–Trotter incidence bound published. Sets up the upper-bound side.

data.Label: Spencer–Szemerédi–Trotter 1984
data.Description: Specialization of ST to unit distances: U(n) = O(n^{4/3}). The ceiling.

data.Label: OpenAI autonomous-AI announcement
data.Description: OpenAI's reasoning model autonomously proved U(n) grows superlinearly (inexplicit exponent > 1), disproving Erdős's polynomial-improvement conjecture and showing the square-grid was not optimal. First autonomous AI proof of a central open mathematics problem.

data.Label: Sawin 2026 companion paper (explicit n^1.014)
data.Description: Sawin's companion paper, posted the same day as the OpenAI announcement, makes the exponent explicit at 1.014 using cyclotomic fields, Golod–Shafarevich tower argument, and Minkowski lattice projection. The structural chain Q(ζ_p) → O_K → Minkowski lattice → short-vector counting → planar projection is what the rulebook's algebraic-tower pathway models.

data.Label: Mid-overclaim 2025-07
data.Description: A moment during the hypothetical-retracted-overclaim's brief validity window (2025-06-15 → 2025-08-01). At this instant the rulebook briefly held an exponent-1.5 claim that was retracted shortly after — useful for proving the bitemporal layer captures transient overclaims.

data.Label: Current moment
data.Description: The 'now' anchor — present-day state of the rulebook.

data.Label: Future improvement (projected)
data.Description: Placeholder for the next plausible improvement — for forward-looking 'will the chain still close in 2030' queries.

LowerBoundValidityAtSnapshot.Description: Table: LowerBoundValidityAtSnapshot. Junction between AsymptoticLowerBounds and TemporalSnapshots — one row per (bound × snapshot) pair recording whether the bound was bitemporally valid at that moment. Pre-computed because ERB's MAXIFS filters on equality, not on date ranges; this junction makes 'as-of' rollups expressible as plain aggregations on TemporalSnapshots.

schema.name: IsValidAtThisSnapshot
schema.Description: TRUE iff the bound's [ValidFrom, ValidTo) interval contains the SnapshotDate. Pre-computed since ERB MAXIFS doesn't support range filters; substrate-side temporal queries can re-derive this if needed.

schema.name: BoundIsCurrentlyValid
schema.Description: Surfaces the bound's curator-set IsCurrentlyValid onto the junction. Distinguishes 'bitemporally valid by date' from 'curator-confirmed valid'.

schema.name: IsCuratorConfirmedAtThisSnapshot
schema.Description: Stricter than IsValidAtThisSnapshot: TRUE iff the bound is BOTH within its valid-time interval at this snapshot AND has been curator-confirmed (IsCurrentlyValid=TRUE). Excludes pending/unevaluated rows from 'as-of' rollups.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Free-text annotation for this row.

data.Description: Updated to FALSE in Loop 5.1: Sawin's bound now has ValidFrom=2026-05-20, so it was NOT yet valid at the 2025-07-01 mid-overclaim moment.
The one moment in time where the overclaim was alive — visible HERE only.

Updated in Loop 5.1: snap-sawin-2026's date moved from 2024-01-01 to 2026-05-20, which coincides with pending bound's ValidFrom; pending is now date-valid at this snapshot (but not curator-confirmed).
Sawin's bound becomes valid on the SAME DAY as OpenAI's announcement (companion paper); the boundary moment when n^1.014 enters the historical record.
Pending bound's ValidFrom=2026-05-20 coincides with OpenAI announcement day; date-valid (but IsCurrentlyValid=null, so excluded from CuratorConfirmed).
